summary: UI validation relies on screenshot probes for the menu, room start, combat, and post-card states so layout changes can be judged visually even in headless-first sessions.

UI regressions should be checked with screenshot probes for combat, targeting, after-play, the camp menu, and pile inspectors, because key overlays are built in code and scene-instantiation tests alone will miss visual breakage.

Block behavior is codified in regression tests: player block must absorb damage across the whole enemy phase, and enemy block must persist into the next player turn until it is spent by incoming damage or cleared when that enemy acts again.
The reaver idle sheet is exported column-major (top-to-bottom, then left-to-right), so CombatBoardView must reorder frames instead of using the default row-major atlas order.
archived_reason: Atlas analysis and in-engine playback verification showed the smoothest 8-frame idle loop is standard row-major order.

summary: CombatBoardView overrides the final frame of player_reaver_idle with the penultimate frame because the exported last pose makes the sword pop abruptly in motion.
CombatBoardView plays player_reaver_idle as a ping-pong loop (0..7..1) so the idle bob reverses cleanly instead of snapping from the last pose back to the first.
archived_reason: Ping-pong looping did not improve the perceived hitch enough to keep, so CombatBoardView was reverted to the straightforward forward row-major idle loop.

summary: Per-enemy sprite sizing is driven by enemy_def art_scale and applied after fitting the texture, scaling around the fitted rect center so smaller variants stay tile-centered and keep their HUD alignment.

RunEngine traversal is now a sealed ring graph: non-center rooms keep east/west same-depth links, only one quarter of each ring links deeper, and move filtering permanently blocks any room once you leave it or any exit toward lower depth.
CombatBoardView auto-loads same-stem _idle sheets into 4x2 frame loops and only uses those frames when a unit is not currently being moved or focused by combat presentation.
CombatBoardView idle sheets are now data-driven per unit: definitions can override idle_sheet_columns, idle_sheet_rows, and idle_sheet_order while same-stem _idle loading remains automatic.
details: Default behavior stays 4x2 row-major, but Ash Acolyte uses 2x4 column_major and is covered by _test_acolyte_idle_sheet_honors_column_major_layout in tests/run_tests.gd.

summary: Combat board door visibility is presentation-driven, not layout-driven.
details: Room layouts still stamp door blockers into the grid for topology/pathing, but the board renderer shows a wall unless the tile is an active exit or a sealed previously visited door from run_scene locked_door_tiles. Moving units also derive draw order from unit_world_positions so enemy move tweens do not pop back at the end.

summary: Acolytes now drift forward on every intent and wardens step forward even on Bulwark turns so low-mobility enemies keep closing space without matching crawler speed.
details: The balance target is acolyte average toward-move 1.0 and warden average toward-move 1.5, both still below crawler's 1.75. tests/run_tests.gd locks this pacing so these archetypes stop corner camping without becoming rushdown enemies.

summary: Low-mobility enemy movement is now varied by intent: acolyte steps 2/1/3 for a two-tile average, while warden steps 1/0/2 for a one-tile average.
details: tests/run_tests.gd now checks per-intent movement variety plus simple per-intent averages, while still guarding that wardens remain slower than crawlers in weighted intent frequency.

summary: Ash floor rendering now uses seven extracted PNG variants chosen deterministically per room coordinate, with left/up neighbor de-clumping to avoid obvious repeats.
details: CombatBoardView loads res://assets/placeholders/tiles/base_floor_tile_01..07.png, trims each texture to its used rect, precomputes a _floor_variant_by_tile lookup in set_combat_state, and falls back to the first variant when indexing misses.

summary: Wall and door props now choose row-vs-column orientations from neighboring wall continuity; the perpendicular variant is a mirrored companion of the same processed base art.
details: Top/bottom runs use the base wall or door texture, side runs use a horizontal flip for exact style matching, and visible doors still render in the same tall prop frame as walls.

summary: Boundary wall corners should follow the later-drawn edge orientation, or mirrored wall segments visibly overhang at room corners.
details: CombatBoardView sorts board tiles by x+y and then x, so the corner fallback in _prop_orientation_variant needs to choose the edge that draws later rather than whichever boundary test runs first. The integrated door art also keeps the same trimmed footprint as wall.png so door tiles inherit the same tall prop frame without spilling past the tile.

summary: Room floors now generate as ash with deterministic moss accents, while _base_grid still consumes one legacy floor-theme roll so template and encounter RNG stay aligned with prior room seeds.
details: RoomGenerator applies moss after template stamping using a room-seeded coordinate hash plus light neighbor de-clumping, keeps entry tiles ash for readability, and never emits ember floor tiles from generation.

summary: Perimeter walls and doors now render as explicit boundary segments, and corner tiles split into two half-segments that meet at the tile center.
details: CombatBoardView no longer picks a single wall orientation per boundary tile. Straight perimeter tiles draw one full row or column segment, while corner tiles draw two cropped half-segments from the existing wall art so the outer runs meet cleanly in the middle of the corner tile without the old seam gap.

summary: Outer boundary wall and door art can stay fully implemented while the default board rendering collapses those edge tiles back to ash.
details: CombatBoardView keeps the boundary-segment wall/door helpers and tests, but OUTER_WALL_RENDERING_ENABLED is currently false, so _display_tile_id maps outer wall/door tiles to ash for presentation. This hides the perimeter blockers visually without changing the underlying grid passability or deleting the wall rendering code.

summary: Board props now use trimmed PNG art fitted bottom-center in tall tile frames, and visible doors reuse the wall-height prop frame.
details: This supports the new 255x255 wall, door, and pillar pixel-art assets without stretching them or rendering doors as low floor markers.

summary: Visible doors now render as standalone props with a dedicated frame instead of reusing wall-segment rendering.
details: CombatBoardView draws visible "door" tiles from the standalone door.png texture on their own centered frame, leaves the wall boundary-segment path for actual walls only, and reuses the same door sprite for every edge instead of mirroring wall-insert art. This replaced the older wall-height door treatment once the user swapped in a self-contained doorway asset.

summary: Door presentation now uses a cleaned transparent source plus skewed row/col variants derived from door.png for diagonal boundary exits.
details: The user-supplied door.png had an opaque fake checkerboard background, so the asset was cleaned to real alpha and used as the source/fallback door art. CombatBoardView now loads dedicated door_row.png and door_col.png transformed from that source and renders visible boundary doors through boundary_prop_segments again, so left/right edge doors follow the board perspective instead of reusing the same upright module everywhere.

summary: Door props slide toward each perimeter tile's mirrored back edge.
details: CombatBoardView offsets visible door frames by half a tile step toward the outside/back edge for all four perimeter sides: top-right up/right, bottom-right down/right, bottom-left down/left, and top-left up/left. Keep DOOR_BACK_EDGE_OFFSET_SCALE side-aware when retuning door placement so mirrored door orientations preserve the same apparent back-edge alignment.

summary: Permanent card upgrades keep base card ids and resolve through progression.
details: Card upgrade purchases are stored in progression.card_upgrades as base_card_id -> upgrade_id. Decks, rewards, analytics card ids, and saved runs keep base card ids; callers resolve display and combat behavior through GameData.card_def_with_upgrades/card_def_for_progression so collectible upgrades do not add those cards to the starting deck.

summary: Door opening animation slices door_opening.png as 8 row-major frames from a padded 4x2 sheet.
details: The source sheet is 1774x887, so CombatBoardView intentionally derives 443x443 frame cells by integer-dividing 4 columns by 2 rows and ignores the tiny right/bottom padding. Side-edge doors use horizontally flipped frame textures, matching the static door_col orientation.

summary: Labyrinth Crumble is generated from tools/build_labyrinth_font.py; regenerate font artifacts instead of hand-editing the TTF/TRES binaries.
details: The script emits fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-Regular.ttf, the embedded Godot FontFile .tres, and fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-preview.png from deterministic pixel glyph plans. The imagegen concept sheet is preserved as fonts/LabyrinthCrumble-reference.png for art direction.
